channel.title: Performance on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Performance on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: dotnet: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot Studio upgraded its WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was described as straightforward, simplified deployment, and improved performance for end users.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description:
The post introduces a .NET walkthrough for adding distributed caching with Postgres on Azure to improve performance, reduce latency, and lower load on downstream systems. It presents current caching best practices and packages them into a reusable application example.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description:
GitHub says the validation tools used by Copilot cloud agent are now 20% faster. The tools include CodeQL, the GitHub Advisory Database, secret scanning, and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Faster incremental analysis with CodeQL in pull requests
item.description:
CodeQL pull request scans for C#, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and Ruby are now incremental, which improves scan speed. GitHub had already sped up pull request scans earlier this year.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL preview updates in mid-March 2026 add changes aimed at reducing storage, I/O, and memory use while improving performance without requiring index maintenance jobs. The provided note is truncated and does not include the full list of enhancements.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster. The update applies when handing work to the agent through supported entry points such as assigning an issue to Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: The Invisible Rewrite: Modernizing the Kubernetes Image Promoter
item.description:
Kubernetes rewrote the core of its image promoter (kpromo/promo-tools), removing legacy code and splitting promotion into distinct phases. The new pipeline improved performance and reliability, shipped in v4.2.0–v4.4.0, and kept user-facing flags and manifests unchanged.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog
